rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical mediator-assisted [4+2] annulation of anethole derivatives with α-methylstyrenes to form polysubstituted dihydronaphthalenes via radical cation intermediates
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of (4-BrPh)3N mediator to its aminium radical cation, which then performs single-electron oxidation of the electron-rich anethole (2a) to generate an alkene radical cation; cathode reduces protons to H2
electron_flow: Anode oxidizes (4-BrPh)3N to its aminium radical cation, which single-electron oxidizes anethole to alkene radical cation I; nucleophilic addition of α-methylstyrene 1a yields distonic cation II, which undergoes intramolecular Friedel–Crafts-type cyclization to arenium III; deprotonation gives radical IV that loses one more electron/proton to deliver dihydronaphthalene 3aa, while H+ is reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline N-oxide)
probe_reagents.observation: EPR signals of mixed DMPO-carbon-radical adducts (g=2.0063, AN=15.20 G, AH=21.50 G; second set AN=14.87 G, AH1=AH2=19.62 G) consistent with carbon-radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, (4-BrPh)3N
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(2a)=1.41 V < Eox(1a)=1.91 V and Eox((4-BrPh)3N)=1.20 V, indicating mediator is oxidized first and selectively oxidizes anethole 2a

probe_reagents.name: no-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed without electricity (Entry 12), confirming electrochemical initiation

notes: Electron-deficient styrenes fail because they cannot be oxidized to a radical cation, supporting the radical-cation initiation pathway. β-methylstyrene gives only the dehydrogenated (fully aromatized) product, indicating IV/3aa is prone to overoxidation. X-ray structures of several products corroborate connectivity and stereochemistry of the proposed cyclization.
